channel.title: FierceBiotech — Health, Longevity & Diet
channel.description: FierceBiotech articles filtered for longevity, health, diet, and healthspan research

item.title: With $1B Novo deal and diabetes ambitions undimmed, Lexicon is in ‘good shape to go crazy’
item.description: Lexicon CEO Michael Exton told Fierce how he set the company's development and financial pathway "on a very different trajectory."

item.title: Fosun pays $60M for option on AriBio’s phase 3 Alzheimer’s asset
item.description: Fosun Pharma has paid $60 million for an option on AriBio’s late-phase Alzheimer’s disease program, securing the chance to expand its rights to the asset in exchange for a further $80 million.

item.title: Medtronic to shutter Santa Rosa plant as it restructures cardiovascular unit
item.description: Medtronic will shutter its Santa Rosa facility that produces coronary stents and other heart-related products over the next two years as the device maker also looks to restructure its cardio business.

item.title: Kyverna begins rolling submission for autoimmune CAR-T as FDA requests more natural history analysis
item.description: Kyverna Therapeutics has officially started a rolling submission to the FDA for miv-cel as the company conducts additional natural history analysis at the agency's request, keeping the candidate on track to be the first autoimmune CAR-T to reach the market.

item.title: Boehringer furthers inflammatory expansion with €407M biobucks bet on Immunitas asset
item.description: Boehringer Ingelheim has put up 407.5 million euros in biobucks to secure global rights to a first‑in‑class antibody Immunitas Therapeutics is developing for chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseases.

item.title: Roche nabs European approval for second Eli Lilly-partnered Alzheimer’s test
item.description: Roche has secured a European CE mark for its second Alzheimer’s test that can both help confirm the disease and rule it out.

item.title: Amid M&A buzz, Inhibrx's OX40-Keytruda combo shows promise in early look at midphase data
item.description: Adding Inhibrx Biosciences’ OX40 agonist to Keytruda doubled the response rate in a midphase clinical trial, encouraging the biotech to advance a program that reportedly attracted the interest of Merck & Co.
